headline: Tech leaders favoured by hedge funds last month, Hazeltree says
summary: Hedge funds stuck to their holdings in companies with ‌strong fundamentals in April, particularly technology ‌and semiconductor stocks, according to a report on Wednesday ​from data platform Hazeltree, in a month when the S&P 500 jumped over 10%.

content.title: Tech leaders favoured by hedge funds last month, Hazeltree says
content.summary: Hedge funds stuck to their holdings in companies with ‌strong fundamentals in April, particularly technology ‌and semiconductor stocks, according to a report on Wednesday ​from data platform Hazeltree, in a month when the S&P 500 jumped over 10%.

headline: Is DoubleVerify (DV) Using Threads’ New AI Ad Controls to Quietly Reshape Its Competitive Moat?
summary: Earlier in May 2026, DoubleVerify announced AI-powered pre-screen content controls for ads on Meta’s Threads feed, allowing advertisers to block unsuitable content before impressions are served and complementing its existing post-bid brand suitability measurement. This rollout deepens DoubleVerify’s integration with a major social platform while showcasing its Universal Content Intelligence engine, which analyzes video, image, audio and text to give advertisers highly granular, automated...

headline: Nvidia Earnings Likely to Shine Even as Stock Underperforms
summary: Artificial-intelligence leader Nvidia has a big task when it reports first-quarter earnings later today: convince investors that the AI investment boom still has legs and that Nvidia won’t lose its pole position.  Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings per share of $1.75, up from 81 cents a year ago.  Nvidia remains the biggest winner of the AI age.

content.title: Nvidia Earnings Likely to Shine Even as Stock Underperforms
content.summary: Artificial-intelligence leader Nvidia has a big task when it reports first-quarter earnings later today: convince investors that the AI investment boom still has legs and that Nvidia won’t lose its pole position.  Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings per share of $1.75, up from 81 cents a year ago.  Nvidia remains the biggest winner of the AI age.

headline: Meta proposes limited free WhatsApp access for rival AI chatbots in Europe (META)
summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) has proposed granting competing artificial intelligence chatbots, including those developed by OpenAI, limited free access to WhatsApp in Europe before introducing charges once usage thresholds are exceeded, according to two people familiar with the matter.

content.title: Meta proposes limited free WhatsApp access for rival AI chatbots in Europe (META)
content.summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) has proposed granting competing artificial intelligence chatbots, including those developed by OpenAI, limited free access to WhatsApp in Europe before introducing charges once usage thresholds are exceeded, according to two people familiar with the matter.
